channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US embassy warns Americans over travel to Trinidad and Tobago as Venezuela tensions flare
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Trinidad and Tobago on Saturday urged U.S. citizens to stay away from American government facilities in the Caribbean country.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Ukrainians disappointed after Trump-Zelenskyy meeting fails to immediately secure Tomahawk missiles
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainians shared their disappointment Saturday that the U.S. may not provide Kyiv with long-range Tomahawk missiles, while work to repair the damaged power supply to the country's Zaporizhzhia power plant soothed other concerns surrounding Europe's largest nuclear plant.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: After air strikes, peace talks between Afghanistan and Pakistan
item.description: ISLAMABAD/KABUL, Oct 18 (Reuters) - Afghanistan and Pakistan were holding peace talks in Doha on Saturday, both sides said, after the South Asia neighbours extended a ceasefire following a week of fierce border clashes.
item.dc:creator: Mohammad Yunus Yawar, Asif Shahzad, NewsNation

item.title: Afghan and Pakistani delegations in Doha for crisis talks over cross-border violence
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghan and Pakistani delegations were in the Qatari capital, Doha, on Saturday, for talks on defusing the deadliest crisis between them in several years, after more than a week of fighting killed dozens of people and injured hundreds on both sides.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One scandal too many forces UK monarchy to sideline Prince Andrew after years of tabloid fodder
item.description: Prince Andrew has been banished from the royal whirl after emails emerged this week showing that he had remained in contact with the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein longer than he previously admitted.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Prince Andrew's antics and scandals have tried royal patience, triggering headlines and suspicions
item.description: Britain’s Prince Andrew was forced to relinquish use of his remaining royal titles after the latest revelations about his relationship with the convicted pedophile Jeffrey Epstein proved one scandal too many for his brother, King Charles III.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Gaza famine claims face mounting scrutiny as mortality data falls far short of predictions
item.description: Experts challenge Gaza famine claims by the Integrated Food Security Phase Classification, citing insufficient mortality data and declining food prices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From barracks to palace: Soldiers who led military coups to become state leaders
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — Weeks of nationwide Gen Z protests in Madagascar sparked by power and water shortages escalated and led to a military coup that forced President Andry Rajoelina into exile. Army officer Col. Michael Randrianirina has been sworn in as the new leader of the Indian Ocean nation.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE GUMEDE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Heavy security after deadly violence in Kenya, as thousands mourn former Prime Minister Raila Odinga
item.description: Heavy security has been deployed to Kenya’s western city of Kisumu before the final public viewing of the body of former Prime Minister Raila Odinga.
item.dc:creator: Evelyne Musambi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UK sees lessons from Northern Ireland that could aid the fragile Gaza ceasefire process
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As Israelis and Palestinians wait anxiously to see what comes after a ceasefire in the two-year war in Gaza, the experience of the Northern Ireland peace process in the 1990s may offer lessons in the thorny process of moving from seemingly intractable conflict to enduring peace.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Deadly floods in Mexico another sign of need for improved severe weather warnings
item.description: Torrential rains in east-central Mexico last week have left at least 76 dead and raised concerns about the government's ability to alert people to severe weather.
item.dc:creator: María Verza, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Drone bombing of police building not a ‘declaration of war’ from cartels, expert says
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – Thursday’s brazen drone bombing of a police building in Tijuana, Mexico, likely was meant as a warning and not a declaration of war from a criminal organization, a private security expert says.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Israel received coffin of missing hostage handed over to Red Cross, prime minister's office says
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel received the remains of another hostage from Gaza on Friday, the prime minister’s office said, a handover from Hamas as the militant group worked to shore up a ceasefire by using bulldozers in a search for bodies under the rubble in the war-scarred enclave.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prince Andrew gives up his Duke of York title as Epstein allegations refuse to fade
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Andrew said Friday he is giving up his royal title of the Duke of York and other honors after his friendship with sex offender Jeffrey Epstein returned to the headlines.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Trump-Zelensky meeting
item.description: President Trump hosted Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday at the White House, part of a renewed push to end Russia’s more than three-year war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: After years of socialist rule, Bolivia’s presidential runoff tests how far and fast it veers right
item.description: EL ALTO, Bolivia (AP) — After nearly two decades of one-party rule, three years of an accelerating currency crisis and too many months of mind-numbing fuel lines, Bolivia is lurching to the right.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and CARLOS VALDEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Polish court blocks extradition and frees Ukrainian suspected in Nord Stream pipeline blasts
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — A Polish court on Friday blocked the extradition to Germany of a Ukrainian man suspected of involvement in the 2022 attack on the Nord Stream gas pipelines and ordered his release, a ruling that was welcomed by Poland's prime minister.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA CIOBANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Orbán celebrates Hungary as 'the only place in Europe' where a Trump-Putin meeting can be held
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungary's nationalist Prime Minister Viktor Orbán on Friday celebrated his country's status as the host of upcoming talks between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russia's Vladimir Putin, a meeting where the two leaders are expected to discuss an end to the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN condemns military takeover in Madagascar as coup leader to be quickly sworn in as president
item.description: ANTANANARIVO, Madagascar (AP) — The United Nations chief on Thursday condemned the military takeover in Madagascar as an army colonel who led the coup was set to be quickly sworn in as president just three days after announcing soldiers had taken charge. U.N.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former security guard at US Embassy overseas is convicted of spying for Russia and Iran
item.description: A former U.S. Embassy security guard in Norway was reportedly sentenced to three years and seven months after allegedly spying for Russia and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, Putin plan Hungary meeting on Ukraine war, Zelenskyy to visit White House Friday
item.description: President Donald Trump schedules call with Russian President Vladimir Putin as Ukraine seeks long-range Tomahawk missiles following recent Russian strikes on energy sites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian barrage causes blackouts in Ukraine as Zelenskyy seeks Trump's help
item.description: Russia battered Ukraine’s energy facilities with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles in its latest heavy bombardment.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Just-released hostage attends funeral of fellow soldier, whose body was among few returned from Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Two hostages released by Hamas were reunited Wednesday in a Jerusalem cemetery for a final goodbye. Surrounded by hundreds of mourners, Matan Angrest, who had returned to Israel just two days earlier, stood before the freshly dug grave cradling his 22-year-old commander, Capt.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel receives remains of 2 more hostages as military says another body was not that of a hostage
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel received the remains of two more hostages Wednesday, hours after the Israeli military said that one of the bodies previously turned over was not that of a hostage. The confusion added to tensions over the fragile truce that has paused the two-year war.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Turkey deports peaceful Christians under guise of ‘national security’ claims watchdog
item.description: Human rights advocate warns Turkey is systematically targeting Christians for deportation, with President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan's policies facing international scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look at some world leaders who have fled uprisings
item.description: A military coup this week in Madagascar forced the president into exile, joining a list of international leaders who fled their countries or went into hiding during uprisings to avoid incarceration or retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Gumede And Mogomotsi Magome, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Parents of 2 US citizens demand return of their sons’ bodies and all deceased hostages held by Hamas in Gaza
item.description: Two American citizens, Itay Chen and Omer Neutra, remain among deceased hostages held by Hamas as terror group violates hostage exchange agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Afghanistan says more than a dozen civilians killed in renewed border fighting with Pakistan
item.description: The countries have traded fire along the border since Saturday, when dozens were killed across multiple border regions.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia makes more precise drones and is using them to attack Ukraine’s vital rail network
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — When Russian drones smashed into the Shostka train station in northeastern Ukraine earlier this month, they killed a 71-year-old man, injured at least eight people and left train cars buckled by fire and riddled with shrapnel holes.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A new form of tourism: US residents seek tattoos in Mexico
item.description: JUAREZ, Mexico (Border Report) – U.S. residents often cite lower prices and personalized attention when asked why they travel to see doctors and dentists in Mexico. The same holds true for tattoo artists.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump meets with Argentina's president on tariffs, bailout
item.description: The U.S. recently announced a currency exchange of $20 billion in pesos for an equal amount of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: No end in sight to government shutdown as House speaker refuses to negotiate
item.description: The shutdown entered its 14th day with the Senate returning from its holiday break, Republican House Speaker Mike Johnson refusing to call lawmakers back to Washington and President Donald Trump’s budget director vowing to keep firing federal workers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Therapist explains what Hamas hostages are feeling
item.description: Dr. Rebecca Bailey joins "Banfield" to discuss the mental process the former Hamas hostages will be going through.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli hostages returning home need space to adjust, father says
item.description: "People need to realize that the hostages that we know so well for two years are not a public property," says Adi Alexander.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Schumer, Clinton give Trump credit for Gaza ceasefire, hostage release
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and former President Clinton gave President Trump credit for the ceasefire in Gaza and the return of Israeli hostages by Hamas. “Today is a wonderful day.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Israel will exile most released Palestinian prisoners with life sentences
item.description: At least 154 prisoners, most of whom have been convicted for attacks on Israelis, will be deported, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to Knesset: Israel has won ‘all that they can’ in military campaign
item.description: President Trump on Monday urged Israeli leaders to turn the page from warfare to peace, telling the Knesset that Israel had won “all that they can by force of arms” and expressing optimism about the region’s future.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9
